codeReview.summary: The reviewed commit satisfies the stated governance-hardening scope. The docs-only classifier is narrowed to the same explicit canonical docs/governance lane described in repo governance (`scripts/enforce_codex_review_policy.sh:39`, `AGENTS.md:29-30`), so markdown-plus-code/config/script packets are no longer misclassified as docs-only. The deleted ballast surfaces (`asyncloop`, top-level `artifacts/*-domain-map.md`, `artifacts/workflow-doc-gap-analysis.md`, `artifacts/security/*`, `artifacts/pr-jacoco-local/*`, `docs/erp-48-parallel-worker-worklog.md`, and the removed 2026-03-26 security report docs) no longer have live references in the current repo governance/docs/script surfaces I reviewed, and the related stale governance links were updated to generic packet-evidence wording. I did not find any blocking or non-blocking correctness issues in the reviewed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The assigned `backend-worker` skill still hard-codes a Java TDD-first procedure that does not fit non-runtime governance/script cleanup features, so the worker had to deviate from the documented process for this feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /home/realnigga/Desktop/Mission-control/.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:32 declares Step 2 as `Write Tests First (TDD)`; /home/realnigga/.factory/missions/7b442fc0-ec79-409b-ba2e-1ff329abb078/handoffs/2026-04-02T15-36-11-814Z__repo-artifact-purge-and-docs-only-governance-hardening__88233512-d6dc-4e50-b9f7-62019f211cba.json:65-74 records `followedProcedure: false` and says the worker used existing governance validators plus policy-classification probes instead of adding Java tests for this repo-governance/artifact-hygiene change.

summary: PASS. The feature correctly removes stale tracked ballast with no remaining live repo-surface dependencies found in the reviewed docs/scripts/config paths, and it aligns the executable docs-only classifier with the narrowed canonical docs/governance lane described in repo governance. No actionable code-review issues found; one shared-state observation notes that the assigned backend-worker skill's TDD procedure does not match governance-only cleanup work.
